title: elaeocarpus altigenus
intro: Elaeocarpus altigenus, also known as the Blue Cherry or Blue Bean Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits associated with Elaeocarpus altigenus include relief from fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or powder, and can also be applied topically as an ointment or poultice to aid in wound healing.
